channel.title: Help: Questions Desk (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Help: Questions Desk" - If you've got questions about the sandbox, chat, formatting (wiki syntax/coding), or anything that involves the writing process and/or something you're currently writing, post here.

item.title: Seeing page comment replies: Re: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't have ANY? How do people have conversations here?

I personally check the Recent Posts listing, but you can also look at the "recent posts and comments" section of your wikidot profile, or bookmark the threads you're currently waiting on replies for. Or ask people to please send you a PM when they've replied.

item.title: Translation: Re: Translation
item.content:encoded:
You're allowed to seek feedback on our forums, I believe. Other members of staff feel free to correct me.

Anyways, you would be better off getting feedback on the INT Wiki rather than here or the French branch. The INT Wiki also has a discord you can join for faster feedback. Once you're done you can choose to post either here or on the INT wiki.

item.title: Translation: Translation
item.content:encoded: I thought about translating an article from French to English (an original SCP in French). If I do translate the article and put it in the sandbox, I am allowed to directly post it on the main site? Or should I seek feedback first (if so, here or on the -FR wiki)?

item.title: Containment question: Re: Containment question
item.content:encoded: If you're writing a humanoid its generally assumed that they will be provided for nutrition and hydration wise. The only time you would specify their needs is when they need something else otherwise, like say for example the SCP in question needed a high-Protein diet, you would specify that.

item.title: Containment question: Re: Containment question
item.content:encoded: Yes, anything that the SCP needs to survive (water, food, clothing etc.) will go into the Special Containment Procedures.

item.title: Seeing page comment replies: Re: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded: They just check back to the replies of places they've commented.

item.title: Seeing page comment replies: Re: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded: It doesn't have ANY? How do people have conversations here? That's not a rhetorical question, literally I'd like advice about, like. What do people do to ever see replies to their posts.

item.title: Containment question: Containment question
item.content:encoded: So Im writing an SCP that has base requirements of supplies (food) to survive… would that information go in the containment section of the article?

item.title: Sandbox Bug: Re: Sandbox Bug
item.content:encoded: Please check your PMs, I will assist you with this query.

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?: Re: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
What Kara said, and in addition:

I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

There are a few things to consider for why this is the case. We get hundreds of new site members every week, and a lot of them want to write for the site but are unfamiliar with the expectations of the voting audience. Also, a lot of the concepts posted are first-time ideas that pretty much always need some editing before a reviewer would be interested in reading a full draft of the material. Some authors never respond to feedback at all. Lastly, a common trend for authors, particularly newer authors, is to stop working on a concept if it receives a negative review, rather than making edits to improve the concept.

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?: Re: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded: That actually did help.

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded: Yes, that helped. That's too bad about Koth, but if I do decide to change I guess I can just chuck some underscores in there or something.

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?: Re: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
They ask in #thecritters for people to give critique, and reach out to many people on the Butterfly Squad Roster. You don't need to get greenlights to publish an SCP, but its recommended you get critique and go through the process because it will help you as an author. Publication isn't strict, its how the viewer base votes that takes out many articles that don't follow the greenlight process.

Not just moderators and admins give greenlights either. Anyone who qualifies for an author page, on crit staff, completed a flight, or chose for the Reviewers Spotlight are capable of greenlighting.

I hope this was helpful.

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
How is it even possible that SCPs are actually even allowed for publication? I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

Were the first couple thousand SCPs published before the publication became so strict?

I'm just not sure if people aren't creative enough, don't think it through enough, or if the moderators are too unwilling to give it a try.

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
How did you determine that Koth was taken?

I went to http://www.wikidot.com/user:info/koth

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
You won't need to change anything in the forums. They will change with your username (And its alright if the staff posts are wrong as they are generally reply's).

Koth was taken by some other user back in 2010. You probably won't get anywhere with the non-existent employees of wikidot and since staff doesn't own it, nobody can just give you the name. Chances are the user isn't even active so they won't change their name if you ask. I think usernames are not case-sensitive, but your welcome to try.

I hope this was helpful

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
Forgot to ask: what about forums? Will my old posts still say Kothardarastrix?

A quick search shows that the only mentions of my name outside "Tales by Title", "Tales by Author", and such are two author posts where I was credited for critting and one time I was chastised for necroposting. It'd be no big loss if those still showed the wrong name, but would they even break if the post itself wasn't edited?

How did you determine that Koth was taken? Are usernames case-sensitive (in which case I might be able to use KOTH)?

item.title: Sandbox Bug: Re: Sandbox Bug
item.content:encoded: Were you logged in when you attempted to create the page? Alternatively, you may need to join the Sandbox 3 wiki in order to create new pages — that page is found here http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/system:join

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
If you've got any pages marked as yours on Attribution Metadata, you'll need to update that page with your new username.

As for Wikidot links… if they're not updated, they won't break immediately - the link will still be there, and it'll still link to your profile page, but it'll still show your old username. However, next time the page is edited, the link will break, and it'll say "user kothardarastrix does not exist." At that point the page will need to be edited manually to update your name to fix it. Sally there's no way of seeing a list of pages that link to your profile, so there no way of knowing for sure all the pages that will break. If you know of any yourself, probably the best bet is to change your name and then update them.

You should know that the user Koth already exists, so if you do choose to change your name, you'll have to pick another.

item.title: Changing wikidot username: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded: My username is sort of unwieldy. I’ve begun shortening it to “Koth” in chat and am thinking of changing my actual username, but I’m worried about the consequences of such. Will places where my name is hyperlinked (Kothardarastrix) automatically change to the new name, or will the links break or something? What about places where it isn’t, like old news pages? Will I need to go back and change those, will they change automatically, will someone else do it, or is not worth worrying about old news pages anyway? Are there any other advantages, disadvantages, or consequences of changing my username that I might not be aware of?
